channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: The Glas Ceiling: Gold, Sapphire and Vaping Surveillance
item.description: When a company built around flavoured vaping products is pushed out by regulation, the impact does not stop at the business itself. It also affects the adults who relied on those products to make switching from cigarettes work. The FDA’s approach to flavours has treated adult preference as a regulatory problem rather than part of why harm reduction succeeds. A market without appealing legal alternatives is not safer if smokers are left with cigarettes. (Published on July 24, 2026byfiltermag.org)

item.title: Full switch from smoking to vaping linked to lower heart risk, review finds
item.description: Heart risk is not shaped by nicotine alone. A review linking full switching from smoking to vaping with lower cardiovascular risk reinforces the central point of harm reduction: combustion is the major driver of disease. The distinction between dual use and complete switching also matters, because public health gains depend on moving away from cigarettes, not simply adding another product. Risk communication should be clear enough to help smokers understand that difference. (Published on July 24, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: How to spend £8 million
item.description: Eight million pounds can buy a lot of public health activity. The harder question is whether it buys better outcomes. Funding should support evidence, transparency and interventions that help smokers move away from combustible tobacco — not campaigns that deepen confusion about lower-risk alternatives. In tobacco policy, spending money is not the same as reducing harm. (Published on July 24, 2026bysubstack.com)

item.title: Smoking Poverty
item.description: Smoking is not evenly distributed across society. It is increasingly concentrated among people facing poverty, stress and fewer routes to support — which means tobacco policy cannot be judged only by national averages. If lower-risk alternatives are made harder to access or understand, the burden will fall hardest on the people already most affected by smoking-related harm. A serious strategy must reduce risk without widening inequality. (Published on July 24, 2026byplanetofthevapes.co.uk)
